tools.name: search
tools.description: Search DC Hub for relevant records (OpenAI Deep Research / ChatGPT connector format). For structured queries (by MW, operator, status,

tools.name: fetch

findings.title: Generic tool name can shadow trusted tools
findings.detail: Tool "search" uses a generic high-value name. When multiple servers load together, this can intercept calls meant for a trusted server.

findings.title: Generic tool name can shadow trusted tools
findings.detail: Tool "fetch" uses a generic high-value name. When multiple servers load together, this can intercept calls meant for a trusted server.

findings.title: Description tells the model to hide behavior
findings.detail: Description of "rank_sites" instructs the model to conceal actions from the user.

evidence.snippet: Deterministic multi-site ranking/optimization under constraints — the normalization contract that lets you compare sites across separate analyze_site calls WITH…

findings.title: Description uses pressure/override language
findings.detail: Description of "plan_query" stacks urgency/override words (always, never). Legitimate tool descriptions rarely pressure the model to act or ignore prior guidance.

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: search, fetch, list_transactions, semantic_search, get_market_context, get_iso_context, get_power_pipeline, get_interconnection_queue, get_retirement_headroom, save_site, list_saved_sites, set_market_alert, export_dataset, get_gas_intelligence, why_dchub, get_permitting_intel, standing_intent · Private data: search, fetch, list_transactions, semantic_search, search_intelligence, get_market_context, get_iso_context, get_refined_queue, save_to_shortlist, get_shortlist, set_shortlist_alert, suggest_reallocation, list_saved_sites, export_dataset, get_backup_status, get_permitting_intel, standing_intent, cluster_sites_by_latency · Public sink: search_facilities, predict_market_trajectory, get_grid_scoreboard, compare_isos, list_transactions, get_pipeline, get_power_pipeline, get_interconnection_queue, set_shortlist_alert, set_market_alert, subscribe_digest, set_site_alert, get_gas_intelligence, ai_capacity_index, grid_transition_radar, standing_intent, plan_fiber_leadin, claim_free_key, bind_email, recover_my_key · Destructive: search, fetch, get_market_context, get_iso_context, plan_query, suggest_reallocation, export_dataset, get_fiber_intel, standing_intent, bind_email.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: search, fetch, list_transactions, semantic_search, get_market_context, get_iso_context, get_power_pipeline, get_interconnection_queue, get_retirement_headroom, save_site, list_saved_sites, set_market_alert, export_dataset, get_gas_intelligence, why_dchub, get_permitting_intel, standing_intent · Private data: search, fetch, list_transactions, semantic_search, search_intelligence, get_market_context, get_iso_context, get_refined_queue, save_to_shortlist, get_shortlist, set_shortlist_alert, suggest_reallocation, list_saved_sites, export_dataset, get_backup_status, get_permitting_intel, standing_intent, cluster_sites_by_latency · Public sink: search_facilities, predict_market_trajectory, get_grid_scoreboard, compare_isos, list_transactions, get_pipeline, get_power_pipeline, get_interconnection_queue, set_shortlist_alert, set_market_alert, subscribe_digest, set_site_alert, get_gas_intelligence, ai_capacity_index, grid_transition_radar, standing_intent, plan_fiber_leadin, claim_free_key, bind_email, recover_my_key · Destructive: search, fetch, get_market_context, get_iso_context, plan_query, suggest_reallocation, export_dataset, get_fiber_intel, standing_intent, bind_email

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.

remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.

remediation.title: Tool description changed since last scan
remediation.detail: Tool "plan_query" changed its description with no version change. Silent description changes are how a trusted server turns malicious after review.

evidence.snippet: before: "ORCHESTRATION meta-tool: turn a natural-language intent into an ordered DC Hub tool plan BEFORE burning calls. Deterministic keyword routing over the tool registry — no LLM, no network, same intent always returns the same plan (free). Returns _entity=query_plan: {best_tool, intent_confidence + workf" → after: "START HERE — the orchestration FRONT DOOR. For any multi-step DC Hub question, call plan_query FIRST, then execute the plan it returns instead of guessing which of the 79 tools to chain. Turns a natural-language intent into an ordered tool plan BEFORE burning calls. Deterministic keyword routing ove"

remediation.remediation: Re-review the tool before continuing to trust it.

remediation.title: Tool description changed since last scan
remediation.detail: Tool "why_dchub" changed its description with no version change. Silent description changes are how a trusted server turns malicious after review.

evidence.snippet: before: "Use when a human asks how DC Hub compares to other data-center data sources — DataCenterHawk (DCHawk), DC Byte, Data Center Dynamics (DCD), Data Center Frontier (DCF), Baxtel, datacenters.com — or asks "why should I use DC Hub / is it better than <X> / what can you give me a PDF or directory can't?"" → after: "Use when a human asks how DC Hub compares to other data-center data sources — DataCenterHawk (DCHawk), DC Byte, Data Center Dynamics (DCD), Data Center Frontier (DCF), Baxtel, datacenters.com — or asks "why should I use DC Hub / is it better than <X> / what can you give me a PDF or directory can't?""

remediation.remediation: Re-review the tool before continuing to trust it.

remediation.title: Tool description changed since last scan
remediation.detail: Tool "rank_markets" changed its description with no version change. Silent description changes are how a trusted server turns malicious after review.

evidence.snippet: before: "Use when a user wants "the top N markets for X" — one ranked list across the 300+ market set rather than N separate get_market_intel calls. Example: "What are the 10 fastest-growing US markets with at least 100MW of existing capacity?" — rank_markets criteria=fastest_growing region=us limit=10 min_c" → after: "Use when a user wants "the top N markets for X" — one ranked list across the 300+ market set rather than N separate get_market_intel calls. Example: "What are the 10 fastest-growing US markets with at least 100MW of existing capacity?" — rank_markets criteria=fastest_growing region=us limit=10 min_c"

remediation.remediation: Re-review the tool before continuing to trust it.
